tech.title: The First Web Server Goes Live
tech.description: Tim Berners-Lee set up the world’s first web server at CERN, marking the beginning of the modern internet.

tech.title: Firefox 1.0 Officially Released
tech.description: Mozilla released Firefox 1.0, quickly becoming a major competitor to Internet Explorer.

tech.title: Windows 8 Smartphones Launch Worldwide
tech.description: Microsoft launched its Windows Phone 8 devices globally, aiming to break into the smartphone market.

history.title: First International Football Match
history.description: Scotland and England played the world’s first official international soccer match in Glasgow.

history.title: Meteorite Hits a Woman in Alabama
history.description: Ann Hodges became the first confirmed person in history to be struck by a meteorite.

history.title: Michael Jackson’s Thriller Released
history.description: Michael Jackson released his iconic album 'Thriller', which became the best-selling album of all time.

world_events.title: Barbados Gains Independence
world_events.description: Barbados officially became an independent nation after centuries of British rule.

world_events.title: Pink Floyd Releases The Wall
world_events.description: Pink Floyd’s rock opera 'The Wall' hit stores, later becoming one of the most influential albums ever.

world_events.title: Surgeon Performs First Partial Face Transplant
world_events.description: French doctors performed the world’s first partial face transplant on Isabelle Dinoire.

births.title: Mark Twain Born
births.description: American author of ‘Tom Sawyer’ and ‘Huckleberry Finn,’ born Samuel Clemens.

births.title: Winston Churchill Born
births.description: British Prime Minister who led the UK during World War II.

deaths.title: Oscar Wilde Dies
deaths.description: Irish poet and playwright known for 'The Picture of Dorian Gray.'

deaths.title: Paul Walker Dies
deaths.description: American actor and star of the 'Fast & Furious' franchise.
